title: Regulatory Affairs Manager - Plant Protection Products
Head of Franchise Immunology
description:
Are you ready to reimagine the immunology patient journey and turn insight into impact for patients and the business? In this senior commercial leadership role, you will own the strategy and performance of the Immunology franchise, translating deep understanding of care pathways and barriers into launches that deliver measurable outcomes.


Reporting to the Business Unit Director, you will lead cross\-functional teams to scale solutions across indications, align local execution with global strategy, and shape how we partner with scientific societies and patient groups. Can you see yourself orchestrating best\-in\-class launches, energising the field, and unlocking growth through innovative value strategies and omnichannel engagement?

**Accountabilities:**

* Therapeutic Area Market Context and Patient Journey: Define the immunology market context through a deep understanding of the patient journey and roadblocks from suspicion of illness to follow\-up; convert insights into targeted interventions that improve experience and outcomes.
* Scientific and Patient Partnerships: Work with Medical and Patient Teams to build collaborations with scientific societies and patient advocacy groups, extending trust, reach and influence.
* Launch Leadership: Coordinate the Brand Team to design and implement pre\-launch and launch activities; drive execution that delivers launch performance across current and future indications.
* Strategy and P\&L Delivery: Develop and deploy local strategies aligned with global direction to achieve sales, profit and market share goals.
* Forecasting and Investment: Build robust forecasts on sales, profit, market share and investment strategy within agreed budgets; pressure\-test scenarios and adjust quickly.
* Cross\-Functional Brand Planning: Define franchise strategy and implement cross\-functional brand plan processes, integrating Company/alliance and product objectives with market access implications.
* Communication and Promotion Excellence: Ensure effective product communication and promotional support; steward compliant materials that enable access and deliver margin and sales targets.
* Market Intelligence and Performance Insight: Lead ongoing market analysis (scenarios, trends, competitors) and monitor quantitative and qualitative product perception to inform decision\-making.
* Cross\-Functional Team Leadership: Lead the Product Business Team to implement, monitor and course\-correct brand plan actions; remove barriers and accelerate impact.
* Field Partnership: Work closely with sales leaders to identify opportunities and issues; convert field feedback into decisive action.
* Innovation and Omnichannel: Leads the digital evolution of the Immunology franchise by fostering a data‑driven culture and championing the adoption of advanced technologies — including AI — to elevate decision quality, accelerate execution and enhance engagement across stakeholders.


Integrates digital insights, analytics and predictive thinking into commercial, medical and go‑to‑market processes, ensuring a mature, scalable and compliant omnichannel approach aligned with global strategic direction.* Compliance and Governance: Manage all activities and account relationships in line with Company Values, Code of Conduct, internal guidelines and applicable Italian legislation.

**Essential Skills/Experience:**

* Responsible for marketing and strategic plans, sales results and competitive performance for Immunology franchise. You’ll have primary responsibility for marketing activities of assigned products to support company objectives
* Define TA Market Context through a deep understanding of the Patient Journey and key roadbloacks from suspect of Illness to pts follow up
* Work with Medical and PTs engagement in order to build a collaboration with TA Scientific Societies and PAGs
* Coordinate the Brand Team in order to Define and implement pre\-lauch and launch activities, driving and delivering launch performance
* Developing and deploying local strategy in line with Global ones to achieve product business’s goals: sales, profit, and market share, both with current and future indications.
* Developing forecast on sales, profit, market share and investment strategy based on agreed budgets.
* Defining Franchise strategy through the implementation of cross functional brand plan process, considering Company/alliance and Product objectives
* Guaranteeing an effective product communication, promotional supports and implementation of Cross Functional Brand plan in order to reach sales objectives and margins, considering also the market access implications
* Assuring the market analysis (scenarios, trend, competitors, etc.) and monitoring the quantitative and qualitative product perception
* Leading cross functional Product Business Team in order to guarantee the effective implementation and monitoring of Brand plan actions
* Working closely with sales leaders to identify opportunities/issues
* Driving innovation in the business finding and implementing innovative approaches like Innovative Value Strategies and omnichannel
* Managing all activities and Account relationship according with Company Values, Code of Conduct, Guidelines/Internal procedures and Italian legislation

**Desirable Skills/Experience:**

* Leadership experience in the Immunology therapeutic area across multiple indications
* Proven track record delivering pre\-launch and launch excellence in complex, competitive markets
* Strong relationships with scientific societies and patient advocacy groups
* Expertise in omnichannel engagement and value\-based strategies
* Artificial Intelligence literacy and Digital mindset
* Fluency in Italian and English; experience operating within Italian healthcare and regulatory environments
* Advanced degree in Life Sciences, Business, or a related field


When we put unexpected teams in the same room, we unleash bold thinking with the power to inspire life\-changing medicines. In\-person working gives us the platform we need to connect, work at pace and challenge perceptions. That's why we work, on average, a minimum of three days per week from the office. But that doesn't mean we're not flexible. We balance the expectation of being in the office while respecting individual flexibility. Join us in our unique and ambitious world.

**Why AstraZeneca:**


Here you will join a commercially savvy, science\-led team that thrives on pace and accountability, working across the full patient journey to deliver solutions that matter. We blend deep scientific understanding with bold commercial execution, start small and scale fast, and use digital innovation to remove friction for patients and partners. If you’re ready to lead an Immunology franchise that turns insight into launches, partnerships and growth, take the next step and shape what exceptional looks like for patients and our business.

description:
Position Title: Trade Marketing Specialist
Location: Vimercate and Monvalle, Italy
Hybrid: 2 days in the office, 3 days remote
Employment: permanent
Come Make the World With Us
It takes great people to achieve greatness. People with a sense of purpose and integrity. People with a relentless pursuit of excellence. People who care about making things better For Those Who Make The World™. Sound like you? Join our top\-notch team of 53,000\+ professionals in 60 countries who are making their mark on some of the world’s most beloved brands, including DEWALT, FACOM, STANLEY, BLACK\+DECKER and more.
The Role :
The Trade Marketing Specialist is responsible for the definition and management of the brand Stanley in Italy, aligned with the product strategy defined from corporate.
The successful candidate will be responsible for managing product portfolios, new product launch, definition of pricing and margins, and developing and executing the trade strategy to drive business growth and profitability.
For this reason, the Trade Marketing Specialist has to develop both product and marketing strategies developing product pricing and promotional activities to ensure the targets are met. He/she handles the launch of new products presentation to relevant stakeholders.
What You’ll Do :* Product Management:
	+ Oversees product lifecycle from launch to promotional strategy.
	+ Conducts market analysis to guide product positioning and pricing.
	+ Collaborates with sales and marketing for product launches and enhancements.
	+ Monitors product performance and market trends for improvements.
	+ Analyzes pricing and profitability to optimize margins.
* Trade Marketing Strategy:
	+ Develops and implements trade marketing strategies to drive sales.
	+ Conducts market research and competitor analysis.
	+ Executes campaigns to increase brand awareness and product adoption.
* Accountability:
	+ Aligns company strategy with key customers and account plans.
	+ Maintains strong communication with stakeholders.
	+ Ensures commercial materials are well\-presented.
	+ Achieves targets, drives growth, and identifies new sales opportunities.


You’re The Right Fit If :* Excellent analytical and communication skills
* High level of self\-motivation and capable of raising enthusiasm in other
* Strong communication skills
* Structured method of working
* Fluent in spoken and written English or almost B2 LEVEL
* Sound knowledge of MS Office (excel and power point excellent)
* Bachelor’s Degree in Business Studies (or similar) with a focus on Marketing
* 1/2 years of experience as Product Marketing / Trade Marketing Specialist


The Details:
You’ll receive a competitive salary and a great benefits plan, including:* Medical, dental, life, vision, paid time off, benefits in support of your well\-being.
* Discounts on Stanley Black \& Decker tools and other partner programs.


And More:
We want our company to be a place you’ll want to be – and stay. Being part of our team means you’ll get to:* *Grow:* Be part of our global company with 20\+ brands to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths.
* *Learn:* Have access to a wealth of learning resources, including our digital learning portal.
* *Belong**:* Experience an awesome place to work, where we have mutual respect and a great appreciation for a wide range of perspectives and experiences.
* *Give Back**:* Help us continue to make positive changes locally and globally through volunteerism, giving back, and sustainable business practices.


What’s more, you’ll get that pride that comes from empowering makers, doers, protectors, and everyday heroes all over the world. We’re more than the \#1 tools company. More than a driving force in outdoor power equipment. More than a global leader in industrial. We’re visionaries and innovators. As successful as we’ve been in the past, we have so much further to go. That’s where you come in. Join us!*We Don’t Just Build The World, We Build Innovative Technology Too.*
Joining the Stanley Black \& Decker team means working in an innovative, tech\-driven and highly collaborative team environment supported by over 58,000 professionals in 60 countries across the globe. Here, you’ll get the unique chance to impact some of the world’s most iconic brands including STANLEY TOOLS, DEWALT, CRAFTSMAN, MAC TOOLS and Black \+ Decker. Your ideas and solutions have the potential to reach millions of customers as we work together to write the next chapter in our history. Come build with us and take your career to new heights.
Who We Are
We’re the World’s largest tool company. We’re industry visionaries. We’re solving problems and advancing the manufacturing trade through innovative technology and our Industry 4\.0 Initiative. We are committed to ensuring our state\-of\-the\-art “smart factory” products and services provide greater quality to our customers \& greater environmental and social value to our planet. We are unique in that we have a rich and storied history dating back to 1843, but that hasn't stopped us from evolving into a vibrant, diverse, global growth company.
Global Benefits \& Perks  

You’ll be rewarded with a competitive salary plus receive entitlements and benefits unique to your country of hire.
What You’ll Also Get
Career Opportunity: Career paths aren’t linear here. Being part of our global company with 60\+ brands gives you the chance to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths.
Learning \& Development:  

Our lifelong learning philosophy means you’ll have access to a wealth of state\-of\-the\-art learning resources, including our Lean Academy and online university (where you can get certificates and specializations from renowned colleges and universities).
Diverse \& Inclusive Culture:  

We pride ourselves on being an awesome place to work. We respect and embrace differences because that’s how the best work gets done. You’ll find we like to have fun here, too.
Purpose\-Driven Company:  

You’ll help us continue to make positive changes in the local communities where we work and live as well as in the broader world through volunteerism, giving back and sustainable business practices.

title: Service & Installation Technician (Heart Failure Division)
description:
Role Overview  

We are seeking a motivated Junior Field Service Technician, or a recently graduated Biomedical or Electronic Engineer, to support technical installations and maintenance activities for our Heart Failure product portfolio. The role involves working in hospital environments and collaborating closely with interdisciplinary teams to ensure high\-quality project execution and customer support.


Key Responsibilities  

Installation and Technical Support  

* Install data communication cabling (optical and hardwired) in hospital laboratory environments according to installation plans and under the guidance of a Service Engineer.
* Conduct pre\-installation tasks for Abbott systems, including delivery verification, unpacking, system placement, and proper management of waste and returns.
* Check and document installations, including serial numbers and configuration details.
* Perform initial functional checks and prepare documentation to support a smooth handover to the System Engineer.


On\-Site Coordination  

* Participate in site surveys together with the Project Leader or Sales team to ensure the feasibility and readiness of installation sites.
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to support efficient project realization and service delivery.


Preventive Maintenance  

* Carry out preventive maintenance (PM) on at least one platform product or MCS accessory product in hospitals or patient homes.
* Coordinate PM scheduling in cooperation with the Capital Equipment Customer Service department.

Requirements


Education  

* Completed German “Meister” or Technician qualification as an electrician, or
* University degree in Biomedical Engineering or Electronic Engineering (new graduates encouraged to apply).

Professional Skills and Experience  

* Ideally, initial experience working in hospital technical environments or installation settings.
* Strong analytical skills and the ability to understand and work with interconnected technical processes.
* Knowledge of relevant laws, standards, and regulations, with the ability to ensure compliance.
* Proficiency in using computer\-based systems for documentation and technical support.
* Ability to work effectively in interdisciplinary teams.


What We Offer  

* An opportunity to contribute to technologies that directly impact patient outcomes.
* A structured onboarding process and continuous learning within a supportive technical team.
* Professional growth opportunities within the medical device and healthcare technology sector.
* A collaborative environment focused on quality, safety, and innovation.

title: Software Implementation Specialist Intern
description:
**Company Description**  

Eurofins Scientific is an international life sciences company offering a unique range of analytical services to customers in multiple industries, with the aim of making life and the environment safer, healthier, and more sustainable. From the food you eat and the water you drink to the medicines you trust, Eurofins laboratories work with the world's largest companies to ensure that the products they supply are safe, the ingredients are authentic, and the labeling is accurate.


Eurofins is committed to providing analytical services that contribute to the health and safety of society and the planet, as well as corporate responsibility to protect the environment and promote diversity, equity, and inclusion across the Eurofins network of companies.


The Eurofins network of companies believes it is a global leader in food, environmental, pharmaceutical, and cosmetic analysis, as well as in discovery pharmacology, forensic medicine, advanced materials science, and agro\-scientific research services. It is also a market leader in certain areas of genomics, clinical trial support, and contract development and manufacturing in the biopharmaceutical field. It also has a rapidly expanding presence in the highly specialized field of molecular clinical diagnostics and in vitro diagnostic products.


In over 37 years, Eurofins has grown from a single laboratory in Nantes, France, with more than 65,000 employees in a decentralized and entrepreneurial network of over 950 laboratories in more than 1,000 companies across 60 countries. Eurofins companies offer a portfolio of over 200,000 analytical methods to assess the safety, identity, composition, authenticity, origin, traceability, and purity of biological substances and products.


In 2024, Eurofins generated total revenues of €6\.95 billion and was among the best\-performing stocks in Europe over the past 20 years.

 **Job Description** **Eurofins BioPharma Product Testing** is looking for a ***Software Implementation Specialist Intern*****,** at EU Level, based in **Vimodrone (MI).**


The Software Implementation Specialist Intern is responsible for the supporting functions of software implementation of Eurofins laboratory solutions. Currently the primary focus is **Electronic Laboratory Notebook** (ELN) software.


Specifically, the specialist intern will work with a team of software implementation specialists to deploy software solutions. As directed by the software implementation manager, will complete the implementation activities necessary for the implementation of in\-house developed software, including but not limited to documentation, ELN template/recipe/worksheet configuration, and possibly training.

**Roles and Responsibilities:**

* Develop strong understanding of software functionality through utilization of available documentation (e.g. User Requirements, Product Backlog Items, Release Notes, Module Functional Definition, SOPs, and Training Materials) and collaboration with software Implementation Manager and software implementation specialists.
* Demonstrated knowledge of laboratory testing operations. Familiarity with common operational processes, use cases, analytical terminology.
* Execute implementation tasks as assigned by the implementation manager and/or as determined by local program plans, including but not limited to:
	+ Change Management documentation
	+ Local procedure updates
	+ ELN template/recipe configuration
	+ Electronic worksheet template configuration
* Execute training sessions as needed, Subject Matter Expert Workshops, and user Question \& Answer sessions utilizing pre\-developed Global Training Materials. Adapt training materials or training approach to effectively address specific use cases.
* As software is adopted, provide surveillance to ensure intended use of the software.
* Provide support and consultation to local Subject Matter Experts in the case of end user questions.
* Demonstrate commitment to quality and the governance of the global ELN program and IT Corporate Standards.
	+ Comply with and promote global training materials and SOPs.
	+ Comply with IT Corporate Standards.
* Strong communication skills
	+ Good listener, and engaging team members
	+ Effective speaking and written skills
	+ Relationship with lab leadership, QA, and users balances between realistic/empathetic and challenging.
* Travel for on\-site visits as needed and available.

  

**Qualifications**  

* Degree in Science, Engineering, or Computer Science or equivalent degree;
* In progress or completed education in Laboratory science;
* Strong personal organization and self\-motivation;
* Attention to detail and interested in problem solving;
* Communication – good listener, responsive, concise;
* Eager to learn and continuously develop personal and technical capabilities;
* Residence near Vimodrone (MI);
* Excellent command of English.

  

**Additional Information**  

* 6\-months Internship;
* Flexible schedule;
* Professional development support.


To apply, it is necessary send a detailed ***Curriculum Vitae written in english,*** specifying the authorization to process personal data. CVs without these requirements will not be considered.


To learn more about Eurofins, please explore our website www.eurofins.com, www.eurofins.it

title: Internship - Junior B2B Clinical Specialist
description:
From day one at Unobravo, we’ve been on a mission to make mental health support truly accessible to everyone by normalizing therapy, fostering openness, and breaking down the stigma that still surrounds it.  

Our success is driven by an exceptional team dedicated to providing high\-quality, accessible care, anytime, anywhere. With over **9,000 qualified therapists** and more than **7 million therapy sessions delivered**, we’re proud to have made a meaningful difference in countless lives.


Join us as our new **Clinical Specialist Intern in the B2B Clinical team**: an opportunity to shape innovation in the mental health industry and drive solutions that make a global impact.  

We’re seeking someone bold and visionary, ready to break barriers and fuel our international expansion.

### **The Essentials**

* Seniority: Junior level
* Languages: Italian, English
* Location: Milan

### **What You’ll Do**


To support our Clinical team, we are looking for a **Clinical Intern** who is passionate about mental health and evidence\-based practice. In this role, you will contribute to research activities and support the development of scientific and educational materials used across our internal projects.


You will work closely with the Clinical team to help keep projects running smoothly and support collaborations with external professionals.


In particular, you will:

* Support the Clinical team with day\-to\-day operational and organizational activities.
* Contribute to **research on mental health and wellbeing topics**, helping analyze scientific sources and synthesize insights.
* Assist in the **preparation and review of evidence\-based materials** for internal initiatives.
* Provide operational support to **external collaborators**, ensuring they have the documentation, guidelines, and resources needed to carry out their work in line with internal procedures.
* Participate in internal team meetings in a **support role**, helping with preparation of materials and coordination of ongoing activities.


This is a **back\-office role**, focused on supporting internal workstreams rather than direct client interaction.

### **Must\-have skills**

* Master’s Degree in Psychology.
* Strong interest in mental health, wellbeing, and evidence\-based practice.
* Ability to conduct research, analyze scientific sources, and synthesize insights clearly.
* Proficiency with Google Workspace and Microsoft Office tools.
* Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
* Ability to collaborate effectively within a team and support multiple stakeholders.
* Proactive mindset and willingness to learn.
* Fluent in Italian and English.

### **Power\-Ups**

* Strong teamwork and collaboration mindset
* Critical thinking and curiosity
* High accuracy and attention to detail

***Not everyone checks every box, and that’s okay. If this role inspires you and you’re passionate about making an impact in our mission, we’d love to hear from you.***

### **What we offer**

##### **Scaling with Purpose**


Join a company in constant evolution, growing fast while staying true to its mission.

##### **Everyone Needs Unobravo**


Your mental health comes first. Enjoy exclusive discounts on psychotherapy sessions with our professionals.

##### **Moments That Matter**


Company retreats, team\-building experiences, nationwide aperitivo parties, and more.. because milestones are better celebrated together.

##### **️ Free Online Language Training**


Free access to our language platform to grow both personally and professionally.

##### **Birthday Off**


Enjoy a day off during the month of your birthday.

##### **‍️ World Mental Health Day, Our Way**


An additional day off to rest, reflect, and recharge.

##### **Inclusive Parental Leave**


Extra days off for all parents, biological, adoptive, foster, same\-sex, because every family deserves time together.

### **To Fit in Our Culture**


At Unobravo, what truly connects us isn’t just *what* we do, it’s *how* we do it.  

If you’re curious to discover the values and behaviors that guide us every day, take a look at our **Culture Page** and see what being part of Unobravo really means.

#### **Come as you are**


At Unobravo, everyone is welcome. We value individuality and believe that diverse perspectives make us stronger.


We’re proud to be an equal\-opportunity employer and to provide reasonable accommodations to ensure a fair and accessible hiring process for all.


If you require any support or adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know, we’ll do our best to make sure you have a positive experience.

#### **️ Selection Process**


Every application is carefully reviewed by our Talent team. If your profile matches the role, we’ll reach out for an introductory call.  

The process may include a case study and meetings with your future manager and key stakeholders, so you can truly understand what it’s like to work with us.  

You’ll always have a dedicated Talent point of contact to guide you through every step.

title: Director, Market activation and Regulatory Affairs
